news.title: Anthropic to challenge DOD’s supply-chain label in court
news.summary: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei said he plans to challenge the Department of Defense's designation of the AI firm as a supply-chain risk. He claims most Anthropic customers are unaffected by the label.

news.title: DiligenceSquared uses AI, voice agents to make M&A research affordable
news.summary: Instead of relying on expensive management consultants, the startup uses AI voice agents to conduct interviews with customers of the companies the PE firms are considering buying.

news.title: AWS launches a new AI agent platform specifically for healthcare
news.summary: AWS is launching Amazon Connect Health, an AI agent platform that will help with patient scheduling, documentation, and patient verification.

news.title: It’s official: The Pentagon has labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk
news.summary: The Department of Defense has officially labeled Anthropic a supply-chain risk, making the AI firm the first American company with the label. Meanwhile, the DOD continues to use Anthropic's AI in Iran.

news.title: X revamps Creator Subscriptions with new features, like exclusive threads and shareable cards
news.summary: X is rolling out a series of new features for creators, like the ability to offer paid threads and other marketing tools.

news.title: Hardware testing startup Nominal hits $1B valuation, raises $155M in 10 months
news.summary: Nominal provides hardware testing equipment to defense tech companies. Founders Fund led the preemptive deal.

news.title: Roblox launches real-time AI chat rephrasing to filter out banned language
news.summary: The new feature goes beyond Roblox’s current text filter, which simply replaces banned words and phrases with the “#” symbol.

news.title: Luma launches creative AI agents powered by its new ‘Unified Intelligence’ models
news.summary: Luma introduced Luma Agents, powered by its new “Unified Intelligence” models, designed to coordinate multiple AI systems and generate end-to-end creative work across text, images, video and audio.

news.title: OpenAI launches GPT-5.4 with Pro and Thinking versions
news.summary: GPT-5.4 is billed as "our most capable and efficient frontier model for professional work."

news.title: Cursor is rolling out a new kind of agentic coding tool
news.summary: Called Automations, the new system gives users a way to automatically launch agents within their coding environment, triggered by a new addition to the codebase, a Slack message, or a simple timer.

news.title: Meta sued over AI smart glasses’ privacy concerns, after workers reviewed nudity, sex, and other footage
news.summary: Lawyers say Meta's marketing materials promised privacy and user control over sharing footage. But an investigation found that subcontractors are reviewing footage from customers' glasses.

news.title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei could still be trying to make a deal with Pentagon
news.summary: Anthropic's $200 million contract with the Department of Defense broke down due to disagreements over giving the military unrestricted access to its AI.

news.title: From Ukraine to Iran, Hacking Security Cameras Is Now Part of War’s ‘Playbook’
news.summary: New research shows hundreds of attempts by apparent Iranian state hackers to hijack consumer-grade cameras, timed to missile and drone strikes. Israel, Russia, and Ukraine have also adopted this trick.

news.title: Why Is Alexa+ So Bad?
news.summary: I stuck Amazon’s Echo Show 15 and its Alexa+ AI assistant in my kitchen for a month. Things have not gone well.

news.title: Posture Correctors That Will Straighten You Out (2026)
news.summary: You’re hunched over your desk and phone for hours. I rounded up gadgets, a DIY trick, and even some yoga advice to help you straighten up.

news.title: Jack Dorsey Is Ready to Explain the Block Layoffs
news.summary: In an exclusive interview with WIRED, Block’s cofounder and CEO says he axed 40 percent of his workforce so that he can rebuild the company “as an intelligence.”

news.title: The War on Iran Puts Global Chip Supplies and AI Expansion at Risk
news.summary: From helium extraction in Qatar to shipping lanes in the Strait of Hormuz, the semiconductor industry depends on fragile links across the Gulf. Escalation could ripple through global chip production.

news.title: KitchenAid Promo Codes and Discounts: Save Up To 50%
news.summary: Save on every purchase with top KitchenAid coupons from WIRED, including up to 50% off countertop appliances.

news.title: Here’s Every Country Directly Impacted by the War on Iran
news.summary: As the conflict in the Middle East continues to escalate, more than a dozen countries in the region have reportedly been affected by air strikes.

news.title: ‘Uncanny Valley’: Iran War in the AI Era, Prediction Market Ethics, and Paramount Beats Netflix
news.summary: In this episode, our hosts unpack the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, particularly as the AI industry has been entrenching itself with the Department of Defense.

news.title: OpenAI Had Banned Military Use. The Pentagon Tested Its Models Through Microsoft Anyway
news.summary: Sources allege the Defense Department experimented with Microsoft’s version of OpenAI technology before the ChatGPT-maker lifted its prohibition on military applications.

news.title: ByteDance’s AI Ambitions Are Being Hampered by Compute Restraints and Copyright Concerns
news.summary: ByteDance’s new Seedance 2.0 AI video model seemed unstoppable—until heavy demand strained the company’s compute capacity and copyright complaints began piling up.

news.title: The 10 Best Shows to Stream Right Now (March 2026)
news.summary: Daredevil: Born Again, The Dinosaurs, and For All Mankind are just a few of the TV shows we’re springing for this month.

news.title: The Controversies Finally Caught Up to Kristi Noem
news.summary: Donald Trump said he would replace the secretary of the Department of Homeland Security. Noem’s tenure was marked by aggressive anti-immigration tactics and ICE and CBP’s killing of two US protesters.

news.title: The Bargain-Basement MacBook Neo Exposes the Insane Price of Another Key Apple Product
news.summary: Can it really be the case that the company can now make a laptop $200 cheaper than a watch?

news.title: Best Workout Headphones (2026), Tested in the Gym and in the Streets
news.summary: Rock your inner jock with a pair of sturdy, sweatproof, and tangle-proof headphones. Here are our favorites.

news.title: How a Music Streaming CEO Built an Open-Source Global Threat Map in His Spare Time
news.summary: Frustrated by fragmented war news, Anghami’s Elie Habib built World Monitor, a platform that fuses global data, like aircraft signals and satellite detections, to track conflicts as they unfold.

news.title: Grammarly Is Offering ‘Expert’ AI Reviews From Your Favorite Authors—Dead or Alive
news.summary: The tool, offered by the recently-rebranded company Superhuman, gives feedback based on the work of famous dead and living writers—without their permission.

news.title: Big Tech Signs White House Data Center Pledge With Good Optics and Little Substance
news.summary: “Data centers … they need some PR help,”President Donald Trump said at the event.

news.title: What AI Models for War Actually Look Like
news.summary: While companies like Anthropic debate limits on military uses of AI, Smack Technologies is training models to plan battlefield operations.

news.title: The Download: an AI agent’s hit piece, and preventing lightning
news.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. news.title: How much wildfire prevention is too much?
news.summary: The race to prevent the worst wildfires has been an increasingly high-tech one. Companies are proposing AI fire detection systems and drones that can stamp out early blazes. And now, one Canadian startup says it’s going after lightning. Lightning-sparked fires can be a big deal: The Canadian wildfires of 2023 generated nearly 500 million metric…

news.title: Online harassment is entering its AI era
news.summary: Scott Shambaugh didn’t think twice when he denied an AI agent’s request to contribute to matplotlib, a software library that he helps manage. Like many open-source projects, matplotlib has been overwhelmed by a glut of AI code contributions, and so Shambaugh and his fellow maintainers have instituted a policy that all AI-written code must be…
